reviewer: Codex independent C4 dataset reviewer v5

episodeReviews.author: GoodMemory C4 dataset author
episodeReviews.rationale: The episode requires three concrete TypeScript formatting changes rather than factual recall. Its hidden IPv6, ordinary-host, and already-wrapped cases follow directly from the first-stage presentation contract and visible signatures; the no-history first stage and pass-to-pass cases are credible controls. The shared registry runner contains no repository-specific branch, while later memory provides the accepted policy context without exposing the target patch or hidden cases.

episodeReviews.author: GoodMemory C4 dataset author
episodeReviews.rationale: Implementing a last-delimiter policy across three exported parsers is real source maintenance. Repeated delimiters, empty tails, and delimiter absence are direct consequences of the explicit first-stage contract and existing return type; the initial no-memory stage and pass-to-pass cases give credible controls. Evaluation is uniformly registry-driven, and later memory carries the accepted boundary rule but not the function-specific implementation or hidden values.

episodeReviews.author: GoodMemory C4 dataset author
episodeReviews.rationale: Writing quote-aware parsing logic for three delimiters is substantive coding. Protected delimiters, doubled quotes, and ordinary single quotes are all specified by the first prompt and exercised without contradicting visible behavior; the frozen failed-approach history, no-memory first stage, and pass-to-pass cases form credible controls. The runner has no repository exception, and remembered policy guidance is useful without containing a parser patch or hidden test answer.

episodeReviews.author: GoodMemory C4 dataset author
episodeReviews.rationale: Correcting three typed parsers requires repository inspection across tasks, shared error codes, ParseResult, and setting unions, so it is genuine coding work. The hidden space, tab, case, and invalid-value checks derive exactly from the prompt and visible union declarations, including preservation of the visible notice member; the rejected-behavior history and no-memory first stage are credible controls. The shared runner is generic, and later memory communicates the accepted normalization policy without supplying the target patch or hidden cases.

episodeReviews.author: GoodMemory C4 dataset author
episodeReviews.rationale: Applying a project duration quantum across conversion and deadline functions is concrete TypeScript maintenance. Fractional, zero, and already-millisecond fields in the hidden cases follow directly from the multiply-once contract and visible field names; stale-policy prehistory, a no-memory first stage, and pass-to-pass cases provide credible controls. The evaluator has no repository-specific path, and later memory supplies only the updated unit policy rather than code or hidden values.

episodeReviews.author: GoodMemory C4 dataset author
episodeReviews.rationale: Slug normalization, ordered CSV deduplication, and path-segment encoding each require an actual source correction, and their hidden cases are discoverable from the self-contained prompts plus visible base behavior. Pass-to-pass coverage and deliberately unrelated documentation-design prehistory make the negative control credible. The evaluator remains generic, and the violet-accent, sentence-case memory is genuinely irrelevant to these string contracts without suggesting a misleading implementation.
